summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #632 where Telegram voice message transcription failures sent placeholder strings to the LLM, causing nonsensical TTS replies instead of graceful error handling.
summary.outcome: Extracted voice handling into a `handle_voice_message` helper that either falls back to caption text or sends a direct user-facing reply and returns early, preventing placeholder strings from ever reaching LLM dispatch. 103 tests passing, clippy clean, 8 new scenario tests added.

repo: The correct pattern for non-recoverable early exits in Telegram handlers is to call `outbound.send_text(...)` and return `Ok(())`, mirroring the existing STT-not-configured branch at handlers.rs:242-258.
Project uses `bd` CLI for task/issue tracking with conventional commit messages; issues are filed with `discovered-from:` metadata for traceability.
Matrix (crates/matrix/src/handler.rs:744-773) and WhatsApp (crates/whatsapp/src/handlers.rs:548-578) have the same class of placeholder-dispatch bug.
Workspace `just lint` has a pre-existing macOS `llama-cpp-sys-2` CMake/CUDA build failure tracked as `moltis-csq`, unrelated to application code changes.
Nightly formatter pinned to `nightly-2025-11-30` per CLAUDE.md conventions.

code.finding: Reference pattern for voice error early-exit: call outbound.send_text() with user-facing message and return Ok(()) without touching LLM dispatch.
Original buggy voice failure branches substituted placeholder strings into body that flowed into normal LLM dispatch, causing ~0-second TTS replies.

workflow: Scoping clippy to affected crates (`-p moltis-telegram`) is a reliable way to distinguish pre-existing workspace failures from regressions.
Parameterized test helpers (e.g., `run_voice_scenario`) reduce boilerplate when covering multiple branches of the same logic.
Filing follow-up issues for sibling channels immediately after finding a bug class prevents silent technical debt.

friction: The voice-handling block was deeply nested inside a large `if let` expression (~80 lines), requiring careful extraction to avoid type mismatches with `Arc<TelegramOutbound>` vs `&dyn ChannelOutbound`.
Multiple read passes of handlers.rs were needed to understand the full structure before editing, suggesting the file is large and densely organized.
Hit rate limit at session end before being able to commit the changes.
open_items: Changes not yet committed — needs a single `fix(telegram):` conventional commit.
Matrix bug: same placeholder-dispatch pattern in crates/matrix/src/handler.rs:744-773, filed as P2 bd issue.
WhatsApp bug: same placeholder-dispatch pattern + missing empty-transcript check in crates/whatsapp/src/handlers.rs:548-578, filed as P2 bd issue.
Pre-existing `llama-cpp-sys-2` macOS CMake/CUDA build failure tracked as moltis-csq remains unresolved.
